title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: pharos graphics package DescToolsX ecosystem. provides comprehensive collection statistical plots, geometric drawing primitives, color tools, annotation helpers, formatting utilities — built directly base R graphics. Building base graphics keeps package lightweight fully compatible everything base R offers (layout(), par(), custom devices), central theme system consistent argument conventions remove usual boilerplate: sensible defaults, automatic margin handling, uniform styling across plot functions. pharos part DescToolsX package suite:

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
code: # development version from GitHub remotes::install_github("AndriSignorell/pharos")

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: large family high-level plot*() functions consistent interface (formula support, grouped variants, theme-driven styling): Distributions: plotDens(), plotDens2D(), plotDensBox(), plotViolin(), plotRidge(), plotBox(), plotECDF(), plotFdist(), plotQQ(), plotProbDist() Categorical data: plotBar(), plotDot(), plotMosaic(), plotCatDist(), plotTreemap(), plotWeb() Relationships: plotXY(), plotLines(), plotCor(), plotAssoc(), plotBubble(), plotHexbin(), plotBag() Special purpose: plotTimeSeries(), plotArea(), plotMiss(), plotPropCI(), plotCirc(), plotPolar(), plotTernary(), plotBinaryTree(), plotFun() Plot methods objects suite included, e.g. plot.Desc.* (desc() results), plot.Lc (Lorenz curves), plot.BlandAltman, lines() methods lm loess fits.

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: plot functions draw defaults central, replaceable theme: Explicit arguments always override theme call site; theme turn overrides package defaults. One place define look — every plot follows.
code: getTheme()                              # inspect the active theme setTheme(list(palette = "Set2"))        # change one component globally setTheme(list(grid = list(col = "grey90", lwd = 1, lty = "dotted"))) resetTheme()                            # back to the default

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: Geometry follows clean two-step design: constructors create geometry objects — circle(), ellipse(), arc(), bezier(), band(), ring(), regPolygon() — overloaded polygon() generic (fully compatible graphics::polygon()) draws . canvas() provides blank, aspect-true plotting canvas, polarGrid() polar coordinate system. geometries plain objects, can transformed drawing (rotate(), transformXY()) combined composite shapes.
code: canvas() polygon(circle(radius = 1), col = "lightblue") polygon(regPolygon(radius = 0.7, numVertices = 5), border = "red")

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: Manipulation: addOpacity(), fade(), darken(), lighten(), mixColors(), grayscale(), colToOpaque() Analysis: contrastColor() (legible text colors background), findColor() (nearest named color) Conversion: hex, RGB, HSV, CMY(K), long integer representations (colToHex(), hexToRGB(), cmykToRgb(), rgbToLong(), …) Palettes: pal() palNames() suite’s curated palettes

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: Utilities handle fiddly parts base graphics: stamp() — automatic plot stamping (author/date), theme-controlled boxedText(), barText(), textLegend(), colLegend() — labels legends beyond text()/legend() errBars(), shade(), splineCI(), band() — uncertainty display axisBreak(), axTicks(), titleRect(), lineSep() — axis title furniture spreadOut() — de-overlapping label positions isValidPlotRegion() — check device geometry drawing

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: fm() / fmCI() — flexible number confidence interval formatting notation(), style() — notation style control .html(), toHtmlTable(), preview() — HTML rendering tables plots, e.g. quick reports

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: Coordinate transformations: transformXY(), rotate(), degToRad(), lineToUser(), abcCoords() Unit conversion engine: convUnit() SI derived units complete str*() family string handling (strTrim(), strPad(), strAlign(), strExtract(), strDist(), strAbbr(), …)

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
code: library(pharos)  # grouped violin plot, styled by the active theme plotViolin(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars)  # correlation matrix plot plotCor(cor(mtcars))  # custom geometric graphic canvas(main = "pharos primitives") polygon(circle(radius = 1), col = addOpacity("steelblue", 0.4)) polygon(regPolygon(radius = 0.7, numVertices = 6), border = "red")

title: Descriptive Statistics Graphics and Utilities
text: Base graphics, grid — lightweight, transparent, hackable Consistent API — lowerCamelCase, uniform argument names ordering across whole suite Theme-driven — one place define look, every plot follows Robust default — automatic margin adjustment, device geometry checks, protected graphics state Performance-aware — Rcpp hood matters

what: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
title: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
text: Returns xy-coordinates text-adjustment values named anchor positions "topleft", "center", etc., used legend.  Useful placing text annotations consistent, region-aware positions.

title: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
code: abcCoords(   x = "topleft",   region = c("figure", "plot", "device"),   cex = NULL,   inset = 0 )

title: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
text: x character string specifying anchor position.  One "bottomright", "bottom", "bottomleft", "left", "topleft", "top", "topright", "right", "center".  Partial matching supported. region one "figure" (default), "plot", "device".  Determines coordinate region used. cex character expansion factor. NULL (default), current par("cex") used. inset inset distance boundary, specified lines text (character heights/widths).  May scalar (applied x y) length-2 vector (x inset, y inset).  Default 0.

title: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
text: list two components: xy list elements x y giving anchor coordinates user space. adj numeric vector length 2, suitable adj argument text.

title: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
text: positioning logic adapted legend. inset computed character units via strwidth strheight, making robust device resizing, font changes, plot-range scaling. Three regions supported: "plot" inner plot area (par("usr")). "figure" figure region within device, accounting par("fig"). "device" full device area.

title: Coordinates for Named Plot Positions — abcCoords
code: plot(x = rnorm(10), type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "")  # coordinates only abcCoords("bottomleft") #> $xy #> $xy$x #> [1] -0.8287469 #>  #> $xy$y #> [1] -3.399057 #>  #>  #> $adj #> [1] 0 0 #>   # place text at a named position xy <- abcCoords("bottomleft", region = "plot") text(x = xy$xy$x, y = xy$xy$y, labels = "My Label",      adj = xy$adj, xpd = NA)  # all nine positions with inset sapply(c("topleft", "top", "topright",          "left",    "center", "right",          "bottomleft", "bottom", "bottomright"),   function(p) {     xy <- abcCoords(p, region = "plot", inset = 1)     text(x = xy$xy$x, y = xy$xy$y,          labels = p, adj = xy$adj, xpd = NA)   })  #> $topleft #> NULL #>  #> $top #> NULL #>  #> $topright #> NULL #>  #> $left #> NULL #>  #> $center #> NULL #>  #> $right #> NULL #>  #> $bottomleft #> NULL #>  #> $bottom #> NULL #>  #> $bottomright #> NULL #>

title: Confidence Interval Objects — as.CI
text: ci object data frame containing least columns est, lci, uci. Additional columns interpreted grouping variables. primary purpose .CI() remove ambiguity ordinary matrices confidence interval data. example, 3-column matrix may either represent three groups columns est, lci, uci. Wrapping object .CI() explicitly declares structure treated confidence interval data. Supported inputs include: Numeric matrices three columns representing est, lci, uci. Data frames containing columns estimate, lower confidence limit, upper confidence limit. Named lists element contains c(est, lci, uci). Results tapply() applied function returns c(est, lci, uci). Grouping dimensions automatically converted grouping variables. returned object inherits class "ci".

text: controversy propriety using discontinuous coordinates plotting, thus axis breaks. Discontinuous coordinates allow widely separated groups values outliers appear without devoting much plot empty space.  major objection seems reader misled assuming continuous coordinates. gap style clearly separates two sections plot probably best avoiding . what: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
title: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
text: can sometimes make sense display data values directly bars barplot. handful obvious alternatives placing labels, either top bars, right upper end, middle bottom. Determining required geometry - although difficult - cumbersome code distractingly long within analysis code. present function offers short way solve task. can place text either middle stacked bars, top bottom barplot (side side stacked).

title: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
code: barText(   height,   b,   labels = height,   beside = FALSE,   horiz = FALSE,   cex = par("cex"),   adj = NULL,   pos = c("topout", "topin", "mid", "bottomin", "bottomout"),   offset = 0,   col = NULL,   ... )

title: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
text: height either vector matrix values describing bars make plot exactly used creating barplot. b returned mid points returned b <- barplot(...). labels labels placed bars. beside logical value. FALSE, columns height portrayed stacked bars, TRUE columns portrayed juxtaposed bars. horiz logical value. FALSE, bars drawn vertically first bar left. TRUE, bars drawn horizontally first bottom. cex numeric character expansion factor; multiplied par("cex") yields final character size. NULL NA equivalent 1.0. adj one two values [0, 1] specify x (optionally y) adjustment labels. devices values outside interval also work. pos one "topout", "topin", "mid", "bottomin", "bottomout", defining labels placed top bars (inside outside) bottom bars (inside outside). offset vector indicating much bars shifted relative x axis. col color used, possibly vector (default par("col")). ... dots passed boxedText.

title: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
text: returns geometry labels invisibly

title: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
text: x coordinates labels can found using barplot() result, centered top bar. barText() calculates rest.  Notice labels placed top bars, may clipped. can avoided setting xpd=TRUE.

title: Place Value Labels on a Barplot — barText
code: # simple vector x <- c(353, 44, 56, 34) b <- barplot(x) barText(x, b, x)   # more complicated b <- barplot(VADeaths, horiz = FALSE, col="steelblue", beside = TRUE) barText(VADeaths, b=b, horiz = FALSE, beside = TRUE, cex=0.8) barText(VADeaths, b=b, horiz = FALSE, beside = TRUE, cex=0.8, pos="bottomin",         col="white", font=2)   b <- barplot(VADeaths, horiz = TRUE, col="steelblue", beside = TRUE) barText(VADeaths, b=b, horiz = TRUE, beside = TRUE, cex=0.8)   b <- barplot(VADeaths) barText(VADeaths, b=b)   b <- barplot(VADeaths, horiz = TRUE) barText(VADeaths, b=b, horiz = TRUE, col="red", cex=1.5)

text: boxedText draws strings given vector labels coordinates given x y, surrounded rectangle.

title: Add Text in a Box to a Plot — boxedText
code: boxedText(x, ...)  # Default S3 method boxedText(   x,   y = NULL,   labels = NULL,   adj = NULL,   pos = NULL,   offset = 0.5,   vfont = NULL,   cex = 1,   col = NULL,   font = NULL,   srt = 0,   xpad = 0.2,   ypad = 0.2,   density = NULL,   angle = 45,   bg = NA,   border = par("fg"),   lty = par("lty"),   lwd = par("lwd"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'formula' boxedText(formula, data = parent.frame(), ..., subset)

title: Add Text in a Box to a Plot — boxedText
text: x, y numeric vectors coordinates text labels written. length x y differs, shorter one recycled. ... additional arguments passed text function. labels character vector expression specifying text written.  attempt made coerce language objects (names calls) expressions, vectors classed objects character vectors .character. labels longer x y, coordinates recycled length labels. adj value adj determines way text strings justified.  value 0 produces left-justified text, 0.5 (default) centered text 1 right-justified text.  (value [0, 1] allowed, devices values outside interval also work.)  Note adj argument text also allows adj = c(x, y) different adjustment x- y- directions. pos position specifier text. specified overrides adj value given. Values 1, 2, 3 4, respectively indicate positions , left , right specified coordinates. offset pos specified, value gives offset label specified coordinate fractions character width. vfont NULL current font family, character vector length 2 Hershey vector fonts. first element vector selects typeface second element selects style. Ignored labels expression. cex numeric character expansion factor; multiplied par("cex") yields final character size. NULL NA equivalent 1.0. col, font color (vfont = NULL) font used, possibly vectors. default values global graphical parameters par(). srt string rotation degrees. xpad, ypad proportion rectangles extent text within. density density shading lines, lines per inch. default value NULL means shading lines drawn.  zero value density means shading lines whereas negative values (NA) suppress shading (allow color filling). angle angle (degrees) shading lines. bg color(s) fill shade rectangle(s) . default NA (also NULL) means fill, .e., draw transparent rectangles, unless density specified. border color rectangle border(s). default par("fg"). Use border = NA omit borders (default).  shading lines, border = TRUE means use colour border shading lines. lty line type borders shading; defaults "solid". lwd line width borders shading. Note use lwd = 0 (examples) device-dependent. formula formula form lhs ~ rhs, lhs gives response values rhs corresponding groups explanatory variables. data optional matrix data frame (similar; see model.frame) containing variables formula. default variables taken environment(formula). subset optional vector specifying subset observations used analysis.

title: Add Text in a Box to a Plot — boxedText
code: canvas(xpd=TRUE)  boxedText(0, 0, adj=0, label="This is boxed text", srt=seq(0,360,20),            xpad=.3, ypad=.3) points(0,0, pch=15)   plot(mpg ~ hp, data=mtcars, type="n", main="MT cars mpg/hp (log-log)",       panel.first=quote(grid()), las=1, log="xy") boxedText(mpg ~ hp, data=mtcars,            labels=rownames(mtcars), cex=0.6, border=FALSE, bg="grey90")

text: character vector length col, containing either light dark, depending yields higher contrast ratio.

title: Choose Optimal Text Color Based on WCAG Contrast — contrastColor
text: function implements official relative luminance definition sRGB colors (including gamma correction) selects text color maximizes contrast ratio according Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.1). Compared simple heuristics (e.g., mean RGB), approach perceptually accurate suitable accessibility-critical applications. relative luminance computed : $$L = 0.2126 R + 0.7152 G + 0.0722 B$$ \(R, G, B\) linearized sRGB components: $$ c_{lin} = \begin{cases} \frac{c}{12.92} & c \le 0.04045 \\ \left(\frac{c + 0.055}{1.055}\right)^{2.4} & c > 0.04045 \end{cases} $$ contrast ratio two luminance values \(L1\) \(L2\) : $$ \frac{\max(L1, L2) + 0.05}{\min(L1, L2) + 0.05} $$ function compares contrast ratios light dark returns better option.

title: Choose Optimal Text Color Based on WCAG Contrast — contrastColor

title: Check Whether the Current Plot Region Is Large Enough — isValidPlotRegion

code: isValidPlotRegion(minPin = .getOption("plot.minPin", c(0.5, 0.5)))

title: Check Whether the Current Plot Region Is Large Enough — isValidPlotRegion
text: minPin numeric vector length 2, giving minimal required width height plot region inches. Defaults option DescToolsX.plot.minPin falls back c(0.5, 0.5) option set.

title: Check Whether the Current Plot Region Is Large Enough — isValidPlotRegion
text: single logical: TRUE plot region least minPin inches dimensions (device open), FALSE otherwise.

title: Check Whether the Current Plot Region Is Large Enough — isValidPlotRegion
text: plot region area inside figure margins, reported par("pin"). size results device dimensions minus current margins (mar, oma), small device oversized margins can render degenerate. graphics device open (dev.cur() == 1), function returns TRUE, since next high-level plot call open fresh device default dimensions. Note querying par() state open device side effect, function deliberately avoids. function side effects never throws; meant guard conditional plotting, e.g. (isValidPlotRegion()) plot(x) else message("device small").

title: Check Whether the Current Plot Region Is Large Enough — isValidPlotRegion
code: # guard a plot against a degenerate region if (isValidPlotRegion()) {   plot(rnorm(20)) } else {   message("plot region too small") }   # require a larger region, e.g. for a wide correlation plot isValidPlotRegion(minPin = c(4, 3)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Association Plot for Contingency Tables — plotAssoc
text: plot based association plot described Cohen (1980) Friendly (1992). cell \((,j)\) represented rectangle: width proportional \(\sqrt{e_{ij}}\) (square root expected frequency) height proportional Pearson residual \(d_{ij} = (f_{ij} - e_{ij}) / \sqrt{e_{ij}}\) horizontal baseline zero represents independence. Cells baseline indicate positive association, cells negative association. Color encodes direction magnitude: diverging palette runs negative color (strong negative residual) white (association) positive color (strong positive residual).

title: Association Plot for Contingency Tables — plotAssoc
text: Cohen, . (1980). graphical display significant components two-way contingency table. Communications Statistics — Theory Methods, 9, 1025–1041. Friendly, M. (1992). Graphical methods categorical data. SAS User Group International Conference Proceedings, 17, 190–200.

title: Correlation Matrix Plot with Theming and Optional Labels — plotCor
code: m <- cor(swiss)  # full correlation matrix plotCor(m, legend=FALSE)   # upper triangle only plotCor(m, triangle = "upper")   # clustered variables plotCor(m, cluster = TRUE)   # with correlation values plotCor(m, text = TRUE)   # customized labels plotCor(m,          text = list(col = "black", cex = 0.9))   # hide grid plotCor(m, grid = FALSE)   plotCor(m, cols=colorRampPalette(c("red", "black", "green"), space = "rgb")(20))  plotCor(m, cols=colorRampPalette(c("red", "black", "green"), space = "rgb")(20),          args.colLegend=NA)  m <- cor(mtcars) plotCor(m, col=pal("red-white-blue-1", 100), border="grey",          args.colLegend=list(labels=format(seq(-1,1,.25), digits=2), frame="grey"))   # display only correlation with a value > 0.7 plotCor(m, mincor = 0.7) x <- matrix(rep(1:ncol(m),each=ncol(m)), ncol=ncol(m)) y <- matrix(rep(ncol(m):1,ncol(m)), ncol=ncol(m)) txt <- format(m, digits=3) idx <- upper.tri(matrix(x, ncol=ncol(m)), diag=FALSE)  # place the text on the upper triagonal matrix text(x=x[idx], y=y[idx], label=txt[idx], cex=0.8, xpd=TRUE)   # put similiar correlations together plotCor(m, clust=TRUE)   # same as idx <- order.dendrogram(as.dendrogram(           hclust(dist(m), method = "mcquitty")        )) plotCor(m[idx, idx])  # plot only upper triangular matrix and move legend to bottom m <- cor(mtcars) m[lower.tri(m, diag=TRUE)] <- NA  # get the p-values p <- outer(   (vars <- colnames(mtcars)), vars,   Vectorize(function(v1, v2)     cor.test(mtcars[[v1]], mtcars[[v2]], method = "pearson")$p.value   ) ) dimnames(p) <- list(vars, vars) m[p > 0.05] <- NA  plotCor(m, mar=c(8,8,8,8), yaxt="n",          args.colLegend = list(x="bottom", inset=-.15, horiz=TRUE,                                   height=abs(lineToUser(line = 2.5, side = 1)),                                   width=ncol(m))) mtext(text = rev(rownames(m)), side = 4, at=1:ncol(m), las=1, line = -5, cex=0.8)

title: Empirical Cumulative Distribution Function — plotECDF
text: base plot.ecdf/ecdf machinery becomes impractically slow well n = 1e7, since tracks every single jump. Beyond thousand points, individual jumps visually indistinguishable anyway, plotECDF() caps rendered resolution breaks points default. Resolution achieved via quantile subsampling, histogram binning: breaks evenly-spaced probability points mapped back corresponding quantiles (quantile, type = 7). rendered point therefore lies exactly true ECDF - unlike equal-width-histogram approximation, adapts automatically shape distribution (resolution wasted near-empty bins skewed heavy-tailed distribution, lost tails).

title: Empirical Cumulative Distribution Function — plotECDF
code: plotECDF(faithful$eruptions)   # large vector - automatically thinned to 1000 points, no breaks= needed x <- rnorm(1e6) plotECDF(x)   # force full resolution regardless of size plotECDF(x, breaks = NULL)   # grouped ECDFs via the formula interface plotECDF(Sepal.Length ~ Species, data = iris)

title: Compute Distances Between Strings — strDist
text: function computes Hamming Levenshtein (edit) distance two given strings (sequences). Hamming distance two vectors number mismatches corresponding entries. case Hamming distance two strings must length. case Levenshtein (edit) distance scoring trace-back matrix computed saved attributes "ScoringMatrix" "TraceBackMatrix".  numbers trace-back matrix reflect insertion gap string y (1), match/mismatch (2), insertion gap string x (3). edit distance useful, normalizing distance fall within interval [0,1] preferred somewhat difficult judge whether LD example 4 suggests high low degree similarity.  method "normlevenshtein" normalizing LD sensitive scenario. implementation, Levenshtein distance transformed fall interval follows: $$lnd = 1 - \frac{ld}{max(length(x), length(y))}$$ ld edit distance max(length(x), length(y)) denotes divide length larger two character strings. normalization, referred Levenshtein normalized distance (lnd), yields statistic 1 indicates perfect agreement two strings, 0 denotes imperfect agreement. closer value 1, certain can character strings ; closer 0, less certain.

what: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
title: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
text: Extracts numeric values character strings using regular expressions. function supports integers, decimal numbers, scientific notation.

title: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
code: strVal(   x,   collapse = FALSE,   output = c("character", "numeric"),   dec = getOption("OutDec") )

title: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
text: x character vector collapse logical; TRUE, extracted numbers per element x concatenated single string. Otherwise, list character vectors returned. output character; controls type returned values. "character" (default) returns strings; "numeric" coerces extracted values numeric. dec character; decimal separator used input. Defaults getOption("OutDec").

title: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
text: collapse = FALSE, list character numeric vectors containing extracted values element x. collapse = TRUE, character numeric vector concatenated values per element.

title: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
text: function detects numeric values including optional signs, decimal parts, scientific notation (e.g., "1.23e-4"). Whitespace sign number (e.g., "- 2.5") tolerated removed returning results. output = "numeric" decimal separator dec differs current system setting (getOption("OutDec")), values converted accordingly coercion.

title: Extract Numeric Values from Strings — strVal
code: x <- c("value = 12.5", "x = -3.2e2 and 7", "no numbers here")  # extract as character strVal(x) #> [[1]] #> [1] "12.5" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "-3.2e2" "7"      #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] NA #>   # extract as numeric strVal(x, output = "numeric") #> [[1]] #> [1] 12.5 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] -320    7 #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] NA #>   # concatenate values strVal(x, collapse = TRUE) #> [1] "12.5"    "-3.2e27" "NA"       # different decimal separator strVal("value = 3,14", dec = ",", output = "numeric") #> [[1]] #> [1] 3.14 #>

text: x object class Style name style, defined either global enviroment options. digits integer, desired (fixed) number digits decimal point. Unlike formatC always get number digits even last digit 0.  Negative numbers digits round power ten (digits=-2 round nearest hundred). leadDigits number leading zeros. leadDigits=3 make sure least 3 digits left side printed, say 3.4 printed 003.4. Setting leadDigits 0 yield results like .452 0.452. default NULL leave numbers (meaning least one 0 digit). sci integer. power 10 set deciding print numeric values exponential notation. Fixed notation preferred unless number larger 10^scipen. just one value set used left border 10^(-scipen) well right one (10^scipen). negative positive value can also set independently. Default getOption("scipen"), whereas scipen=0 overridden. bigMark character; empty used mark every 3 decimals decimal point. Default "" (none). decMark character, specifying decimal mark used. provided, default set decMark option used. naForm character, string specifying NAs specially formatted.  set NULL (default) special action taken. zeroForm character, string specifying zeros specially formatted. Useful pretty printing 'sparse' objects.  set NULL (default) special action taken. fmt either format string, allowing flexibly define special formats object class style, consisting list fdm arguments. See Details. pThreshold numerical tolerance used mainly formatting p values, less pThreshold formatted "\code{< [pThreshold]}" ('[pThreshold]' stands format(pThreshold, digits)).  Default 0.001. width integer, defined fixed width strings. align character whose position strings aligned. Left alignment can requested setting sep = "\l", right alignment "\r" center alignment "\c". Mind backslashes, omitted, strings aligned character l, r c respectively. default NULL just leave strings . argument send directly function strAlign() argument sep. lang optional value setting language months daynames. Can either "local" current locale "en" english. left NULL, DescToolsOption "lang" searched found "local" taken default. label description style ... arguments passed methods.

title: Format Styles — style
text: style() can either create new styles edit existing ones. style() can used create new styles. takes arguments fm() combines object class "Style", can handed fm() argument fmt.  Following define new format template named "num.sty".  Passed fm() result number displayed 2 fixed digits comma big mark: result arguments supplied directly, helps avoid boilerplate code: fm(12222.89345, digits=2, bigMark=","). edit style can provide style() name overwrite, resp. add new format options.  style("num.sty", digits=1, sci=10) use current version numeric format change digits 1 threshold switch scientifc presentation numbers > 1e10 < 1e-10. styles() returns found style definitions global environment options. styles can stored options convenience.  store new format use default options() approach: options(num.sty = style(digits=1, bigMark=" ")) Defined styles options can passed fm() simply name. Many report functions (e.g. tOne()) DescToolsX use three default formats counts (named "abs.sty"), numeric values ("num.sty") percentages ("per.sty").

code: # use style() to get and define new formats stored as option num.sty <- style(digits=2, bigMark=" ") abs.sty <- style(digits=0, bigMark=" ") dat.sty <- style(fmt="MM, dd yyyy")  num.sty                             # displays the details of the style #> Definition:    digits=2, bigMark=" " #> Example:       314 159.27 # editing styles style("abs.sty")                    # looks for format "abs.sty" #> Definition:    digits=0, big.mark="", label="Number format for counts" #> Example:       314159 #> (Source:       options) #>  # style("nexist")                     # return for nonexisting style style("abs.sty", bigMark="")       # get Style("abs") and overwrite bigMark #> Definition:    digits=0, big.mark="", label="Number format for counts", bigMark="" #> Example:       314159 #> (Source:       options) #>  style("abs.sty", naForm="-")       # get Style("abs") and add user defined naForm #> Definition:    digits=0, big.mark="", label="Number format for counts", naForm="-" #> Example:       314159 #> (Source:       options) #>   styles()                            # all defined formats #> $abs.sty #> Definition:    digits=0, big.mark="", label="Number format for counts" #> Example:       314159 #> (Source:       options) #>  #> $num.sty #> Definition:    digits=3, big.mark="", label="Number format for numeric values" #> Example:       314159.265 #> (Source:       options) #>  #> $per.sty #> Definition:    digits=1, fmt="%", name="per", label="Percentage number format" #> Example:       31415926.5% #> (Source:       options) #>  #> $pval.sty #> Definition:    fmt="p", eps=0.001, label="Number format for p-values" #> Example:       NA #> (Source:       options) #>  styles()[c("num.sty", "abs.sty")]   # numeric and integer styles #> $num.sty #> Definition:    digits=3, big.mark="", label="Number format for numeric values" #> Example:       314159.265 #> (Source:       options) #>  #> $abs.sty #> Definition:    digits=0, big.mark="", label="Number format for counts" #> Example:       314159 #> (Source:       options) #>   # define totally new format and store as option options(nob.sty=style(digits=5, naForm="nodat"))  # using styles fm(314.1563, fmt=abs.sty) #> [1] 314 fm(314.1563, fmt=num.sty) #> [1] 314.16  fm(Sys.Date(), fmt=dat.sty) #> [1] 07, 20 2026

what: Direct Labels in the Right Margin — textLegend
title: Direct Labels in the Right Margin — textLegend

code: textLegend(   y,   labels = names(y),   line = c(1, 1),   width = 1,   col = par("fg"),   lty = par("lty"),   lwd = par("lwd"),   cex = par("cex"),   main = NULL,   mindist = NULL )

title: Direct Labels in the Right Margin — textLegend
text: y numeric vector vertical anchor positions user coordinates, typically last observed value series, series (column) order. Sorting collision handling done internally. labels character vector labels, parallel y. Defaults names(y), series index y unnamed. line numeric vector length 1 2 (recycled), margin lines side 4. first element offset segments plot region, second gap segments labels. width length line segments margin lines. Set NA suppress segments; labels placed directly line[1]. col, lty, lwd color, line type width segments, parallel y (series order, recycled). Ignored width NA. cex character expansion labels. Also enters default vertical spacing, larger text automatically gets wider spacing. main optional title legend, drawn top plot region. Default NULL (none). mindist minimal vertical distance labels user coordinates, passed spreadOut(). Default 1.2 * strheight("M") * cex.

title: Direct Labels in the Right Margin — textLegend
text: (sorted spread) vertical label positions, invisibly. Useful adding annotation next labels.

title: Direct Labels in the Right Margin — textLegend
text: function owns legend geometry: anchor positions y sorted internally legend follows vertical order lines, graphical parameters recycled accordingly, vertical label collisions resolved via spreadOut(). Callers therefore simply pass values series order, parallel col, lty lwd. Drawing uses mtext() segments() device margin; xpd set TRUE restored exit. Horizontal positions given margin lines side 4 converted user coordinates via lineToUser(). Make sure right margin wide enough segments labels, e.g. via mar argument calling plot function.

what: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: getTheme(), setTheme(), resetTheme() user-facing entry points pharos's theme system: single, named list graphical formatting defaults, consulted essentially every plotting function package (fm() numeric/percentage/p-value formatting) whenever corresponding function argument left default value.

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
code: getTheme()  setTheme(theme)  resetTheme()

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: theme either named list theme components merge active theme (given top-level elements replaced; e.g. setTheme(list(palette = "Set2")) changes palette, leaving grid, box, twin, etc. untouched), single string naming preset registered (currently empty) preset registry.

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: getTheme() resetTheme() return (new) active theme, named list; setTheme() returns new active theme well, invisibly.

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: graphical parameters pharos's plotting functions (col, grid, box, pch, ...) default sentinel value, .useTheme, rather hardcoded color number. call time, sentinel resolved getTheme() - changing active theme changes look every plot produced afterwards explicitly override argument, without touching single plotting function's code. gives three independent ways control plot's appearance, order precedence (highest first): Explicit argument, e.g. plotXY(x, y, col = "red") - always wins, one call . Function-specific default - functions deliberately hardcode value differs generic theme baseline instead using .useTheme (e.g. plotBar()'s box defaults FALSE rather theme's box setting, plotDot()'s grid line color/style stays orange/grey dashed look regardless active theme). conscious per-function design choice, bug - see individual function's documentation arguments opt theme-following way. Active theme (getTheme()) - package-wide fallback used whenever neither applies.

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: theme named list following top-level components. Nested components (grid, box, points, bar, sty) named lists; setTheme() replaces wholesale (merge one level deeper), change single nested value, supply complete sub-list, e.g. setTheme(list(grid = list(col = "red", lwd = 1, lty = "dotted"))). par col.axis="grey40", las=1, cex=1.1. Global par() pass applied every .applyParFromDots() call (axis label color, axis label orientation, global text scaling). grid col="grey80", lwd=1, lty="dotted", plus group.* variants. Background grid lines, via .drawGrid(). group.* entries style subordinate/secondary grid (e.g. group separators) function draws one. box col="grey50", lwd=1, lty="solid". frame drawn around plot region, via .drawBox(). points pch=21, col="grey50",     bg=addOpacity("grey"), cex=1.1. Default point styling scatterplot-like functions (e.g. plotXY(), plotDot()). twin pal("helsana")[c(6, 1)]. fixed pair colors contexts inherently need exactly two contrasting colors (e.g. fit line smoother plotXY(), two poles diverging color ramp plotCor(), single accent color via twin[1] lines.loess/plotQQ()'s confidence band). Never used substitute palette two colors needed. palette "helsana". Name qualitative (categorical) palette used whenever two unordered colors needed (e.g. plotMosaic()'s fill colors), resolved via pal(). Deliberately used sequential diverging numeric scales – see next item. (none – design) Sequential/diverging numeric color scales (e.g. plotDens2D()'s density heatmap, plotHeatmap()'s cell shading) deliberately theme-driven; use hardcoded, purpose-built palette via pal() instead (e.g. pal("red-black"), pal("Blues")). Neither palette (categorical) twin (fixed pair) right semantic fit ordered, continuous scale – see pal registry named continuous palettes. bar col="grey80", border=NA. Default bar fill/border plotBar(). sty abs="abs.sty", perc="per.sty", num="num.sty",     pval="pval.sty". Names fm() format styles (see styles()) used absolute counts, percentages, plain numbers, p-values respectively. stamp expression(...) – unevaluated. corner stamp text drawn every stamp()/.withGraphicsState() call. Stored unevaluated expression() eval()'d draw time (theme-load theme-set time), always reflects current user date rather freezing whatever theme defined last changed. Default: "<username> / <YYYY-MM-DD>".

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: Internally, function argument follow active theme (rather fixed value) defaults dedicated sentinel object, .useTheme, TRUE, FALSE, NA, NULL. four frequently legitimate, explicit values right (e.g. grid = NULL commonly means "suppress grid" given function) - using also mean "value given, defer theme" make case ambiguous. dedicated sentinel avoids ambiguity entirely keeps resolution logic single, explicit equality check (identical(x, .useTheme)) rather implicit, error-prone guess based data type. Two small internal helpers resolve : .useThemeValue(value, ...) - simple value taken nested theme key, e.g. col <- .useThemeValue(col, "points", "col"). .resolveToggle(spec, themeValue) - /-style argument (grid/box) may also TRUE/FALSE/NA/NULL/list spec right, used internally .drawGrid()/.drawBox(). theme values require simple key lookup resolve (e.g. building color ramp twin, constructing list point parameters points); resolved small inline identical(x, .useTheme) check directly consuming function rather forcing one two generic helpers . See e.g. plotCor()'s col argument plotDot()'s pch argument worked examples.

what: How plotting functions consume the theme
title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: .applyParFromDots() Applies getTheme()$par lowest-precedence par() pass, function's defaults user's .... .drawGrid(grid, defaults = list()) / .drawBox(box, defaults = list()) Generic dispatchers wrapping graphics::grid()/ graphics::box(). Resolve .useTheme sentinel, merge theme's style values function-specific defaults (e.g. plotBar() suppressing axis-parallel grid direction via nx/ny), dispatch via callIf. used every function draws grid frame: (plotCor(), plotHeatmap(), plotDot()) grid/box geometry tied exact data coordinates (e.g. half-integer cell boundaries) match graphics::grid()'s axis-tick-based geometry: resolve theme values directly via getTheme()$grid/ getTheme()$box draw clipped rect()/abline() calls instead. .withGraphicsState(expr, stamp = .useTheme, ...) Wraps plotting function's body, restores par() afterwards, - expr run successfully - calls stamp() (possibly theme-resolved) corner stamp text/arguments.

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
text: setTheme() also accepts single preset name (string) instead list, looked internal preset registry (.themePresets). presets currently registered; registry exists named, complete theme variants (e.g. monochrome high-contrast theme) can added later without changing setTheme() interface.

title: pharos's Graphics and Formatting Theme — theme
code: # inspect the active theme getTheme() #> $par #> $par$col.axis #> [1] "grey40" #>  #> $par$las #> [1] 1 #>  #> $par$cex #> [1] 1.1 #>  #>  #> $grid #> $grid$col #> [1] "grey80" #>  #> $grid$lwd #> [1] 1 #>  #> $grid$lty #> [1] "dotted" #>  #> $grid$group.col #> [1] "grey50" #>  #> $grid$group.lwd #> [1] 1 #>  #> $grid$group.lty #> [1] "dotted" #>  #>  #> $box #> $box$col #> [1] "grey50" #>  #> $box$lwd #> [1] 1 #>  #> $box$lty #> [1] "solid" #>  #>  #> $points #> $points$pch #> [1] 21 #>  #> $points$col #> [1] "grey50" #>  #> $points$bg #>        grey  #> "#BEBEBE80"  #>  #> $points$cex #> [1] 1.1 #>  #>  #> $twin #>       red      blue  #> "#9A0941" "#8296C4"  #>  #> $palette #> [1] "helsana" #>  #> $bar #> $bar$col #> [1] "grey80" #>  #> $bar$border #> [1] NA #>  #>  #> $legend #> $legend$bg #>       white  #> "#FFFFFF80"  #>  #> $legend$box.col #> [1] "grey50" #>  #>  #> $sty #> $sty$abs #> Definition:    digits=0, bigMark="", label="Number format for counts" #> Example:       314159 #>  #> $sty$perc #> Definition:    digits=1, fmt="%", label="Percentage number format" #> Example:       31415926.5% #>  #> $sty$num #> Definition:    digits=3, bigMark="", label="Number format for numeric values" #> Example:       314159.265 #>  #> $sty$pval #> Definition:    fmt="p", pThreshold=0.001, label="Number format for p-values" #> Example:       NA #>  #>  #> $stamp #> expression(gettextf("%s / %s", Sys.getenv("USERNAME"), format(Sys.Date(),  #>     "%Y-%m-%d"))) #>   # change only the qualitative palette for the rest of the session setTheme(list(palette = "Set2"))  # change the accent color pair used for e.g. lm/loess fit lines setTheme(list(twin = c("firebrick", "navy")))  # turn grid lines off package-wide (any .useTheme-driven grid argument # everywhere now resolves to "off", unless a function overrides it) setTheme(list(grid = FALSE))  # back to package defaults resetTheme()
